title: BOM Decomposition — NAICS 493120 Refrigerated Warehousing & Storage (SAM)

sections.title: Routing Note — Why No End-Product BOM
sections.content:
| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| NAICS sector | 49 (Transportation & Warehousing) — **service sector** |
| Is output physical? | NO — output is a storage *service*, not a manufactured product |
| Component doc routing rule | Service/Ecosystem NAICS: "Use service composition instead of BOM" |
| Does Mein Solar product end up IN the warehouse output? | NO — BESS is an on-site capital asset, not embedded in the cold-chain goods |
| End-product BOM produced? | **NO** — forcing a BOM on a refrigerated-warehouse "output" creates phantom entities |
| VN System BOM produced? | **YES** — the BESS asset itself, which IS the VN L5 system Mein Solar delivers at the site |

**This document decomposes BOM 1: the commissioned BESS plant** (Mein Solar's L5 deliverable at a cold-storage site). Refrigeration-plant, racking, and cold-chain-goods BOMs are out of scope — Mein Solar's product is not a material input to them.

---

sections.title: Company Position Marking
sections.content:
```
L5  Commissioned BESS Plant (4.5 MW / 9 MWh class)        ← [Mein Solar] position (EPC integrator, delivers L5)
 |
 +-- L4  Battery storage subsystem
 +-- L4  Power conversion subsystem (PCS)
 +-- L4  Grid coupling & switchgear subsystem
 +-- L4  Control & EMS subsystem
 +-- L4  Containment / enclosure subsystem
 +-- L4  Thermal management subsystem
 +-- L4  Fire safety subsystem
 +-- L4  Civil / site subsystem
```

Mein Solar = **L5 integrator**. It owns the system-level deliverable, procures L4 sub-systems, does not manufacture L1/L0 commodities. Marked at L5.

---

sections.title: B.1 — L4 Subsystems
sections.content:
| # | L4 Subsystem | Function category | Required for cold-storage BESS? |
|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Battery storage subsystem | Primary functional core | YES |
| S2 | Power conversion subsystem (PCS) | Primary functional core | YES |
| S3 | Grid coupling & switchgear | Control / interface | YES (MV site) |
| S4 | Control & EMS subsystem | Control / interface | YES |
| S5 | Containment / enclosure | Packaging | YES |
| S6 | Thermal management | Auxiliary / support | YES |
| S7 | Fire safety subsystem | Auxiliary / support | YES |
| S8 | Civil / site subsystem | Auxiliary / support | YES |

### L5 → L4 Alternatives Table (Subsystem Selection)

```
L5 Commissioned BESS Plant → L4 Subsystem composition

+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Alternative architecture                    | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Containerized AC-coupled (all 8 subsystems) | 80%   | → Stable      | HIGH       | industry norm, DNV 2024 |
| Skid-mounted AC-coupled (no container)      | 10%   | ↓ Declining   | MEDIUM     | analyst estimate        |
| DC-coupled with PV (merged PCS)             |  8%   | ↑ Growing     | MEDIUM     | pv-magazine 2025        |
| Building-integrated rack (no ISO enclosure) |  2%   | → Stable      | LOW        | analyst estimate        |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

---

sections.title: B.2 + B.3 — Full L4 → L0 Decomposition with Alternatives
sections.content:
### S1. Battery Storage Subsystem — L4 → L0

**L4 → L3**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Module architecture                         | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| LFP prismatic rack (280–314 Ah cells)       | 85%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Wood Mackenzie 2025     |
| NMC prismatic/pouch rack                    | 12%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | Wood Mackenzie 2025     |
| LFP cylindrical (46XX format)               |  2%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | analyst estimate        |
| Sodium-ion rack                             |  1%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | CATL/HiNa 2025 releases |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L3 modules:** Battery Rack Module · BMS/BAMS/BCMS Module · DC bus & combiner Module · Rack thermal interface Module

**L3 → L2 — Battery Rack Module**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Rack assembly type                          | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Modular pack rack (8–16 packs/rack)         | 90%   | → Stable      | HIGH       | container OEM standard  |
| Blade-cell rack (BYD Blade)                 |  7%   | ↑ Growing     | MEDIUM     | BYD 2024–25 shipments   |
| Immersion-cooled rack                       |  3%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | XING Mobility, Sungrow  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L2 assemblies:** Battery pack · Rack frame · Bus-bar assembly · Pack-level BMS (slave) · DC disconnect

**L2 → L1 — Battery Pack**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Pack format                                 | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| 52–96-cell prismatic pack (LFP 280/314 Ah)  | 88%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | CATL/EVE/REPT 2025      |
| Pouch-cell module (NMC, 12–24 cells)        |  9%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | LG ES, SDI shipments    |
| Cylindrical module (21700/46XX)             |  3%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | analyst estimate        |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L1 parts:** Prismatic cell · Cell bus-bar · Cold-plate liner · Pack housing · Internal BMS slave · Pack-end terminals · Compression foam

**L1 → L0 — Prismatic LFP Cell (the dominant L1)**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Active cathode material                     | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| LFP (LiFePO₄)                               | 85%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Wood Mackenzie 2025     |
| NMC 811 / NMC 622                           | 13%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | IEA GCM Outlook 2025    |
| LMFP (manganese-enriched LFP)               |  1%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | Gotion 2025 release     |
| Sodium (layered oxide / Prussian blue)      |  1%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | CATL 2nd-gen Na         |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```
**Other L0 commodities per L1:** Aluminum cell can (`Al 1070`, 100%, single-source: true) · Copper current collector (100%) · Electrolyte (LiPF₆ carbonate blend, 95%) · Polyolefin separator (ceramic-coated 85% / uncoated 15%) · Graphite anode (synthetic 70% / natural 30%).

### S2. Power Conversion Subsystem (PCS) — L4 → L0

**L4 → L3 — PCS Topology**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| PCS architecture                            | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Central PCS (1.5–5 MW skid)                 | 55%   | → Stable      | HIGH       | S&P Global 2025         |
| String PCS (modular 125–350 kW)             | 38%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Sungrow, Huawei LUNA    |
| Modular DC-block (DC-coupled PV)            |  7%   | ↑ Emerging    | MEDIUM     | pv-magazine 2025        |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L3 modules:** Power stage module · DC-link capacitor bank · LCL output filter · Auxiliary DC supply · PCS controller

**L3 → L2 — Power Stage Module**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Switching device                            | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Silicon IGBT (1700 V)                       | 78%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | Yole Power SiC/IGBT 2025|
| SiC MOSFET (1700–3300 V)                    | 20%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Yole, Infineon roadmap  |
| GaN HEMT                                    |  2%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | analyst estimate        |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L2 assemblies:** IGBT/SiC half-bridge assembly · Gate driver assembly · DC-link capacitor bank · LCL filter inductor · Output contactor · Heatsink assembly

**L2 → L1 — Half-Bridge Power Module**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Packaging                                   | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| PrimePACK / EconoDUAL IGBT module           | 70%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | Infineon, Semikron      |
| SiC discrete / half-bridge module           | 25%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Wolfspeed, onsemi       |
| Press-pack IGBT (high-power)                |  5%   | → Stable      | MEDIUM     | Hitachi, Mitsubishi     |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L1 parts:** IGBT/SiC die · DBC ceramic substrate · Wire bonds · Molded epoxy case · Copper base plate · Thermal interface pad · Capacitor can · LCL inductor core · Contactor

**L1 → L0 — IGBT/SiC Die**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Semiconductor substrate                     | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Silicon (float-zone / CZ)                   | 78%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | SEMI 2025               |
| Silicon carbide (4H-SiC)                    | 20%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | Wolfspeed, STMicro      |
| Gallium nitride                             |  2%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | analyst estimate        |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```
**Other L0 per L1:** Aluminum oxide DBC ceramic (80%) / AlN (20%) · Aluminum wire bonds (60%) / copper (40%) · Electrolytic Al can (100%, single-source: true) · CRGO ferrite / silicon steel inductor core.

### S7. Fire Safety — L4 → L0

**L4 → L3**
```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Fire-suppression architecture               | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+

| Water-mist + clean-agent hybrid             | 20%   | ↑ Growing     | MEDIUM     | DNV GL, Fike hybrid     |
| Inert-gas (IG-100 / IG-541)                 |  8%   | → Stable      | MEDIUM     | niche, dense-enclosure  |

```

```
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Wall type                                   | Share | Trend         | Confidence | Source                  |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Precast concrete REI120 panel               | 70%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | VdS 3103, Feuerwehr req |
| Masonry F120 wall                           | 20%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | legacy                  |
| Steel stud + cement-board F90               | 10%   | → Stable      | MEDIUM     | cost-driven             |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

**L2 assemblies:** Concrete slab · Rebar cage · Formwork · MV cable · LV cable · Fence panel · Gate · Earthing mat

**L2 → L1 — Concrete Slab → L1 Parts:** Rebar · Formwork tie · Anchor bolt · Aggregate mix · Cement paste

**L1 → L0**
```
Rebar:
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| B500B reinforcing steel (EN 10080)          | 97%   | → Stable      | HIGH       | EN 10080 DE standard    |
| GFRP rebar (glass-fiber)                    |  3%   | ↑ Emerging    | LOW        | corrosion-sensitive     |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+

Concrete:
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| OPC (CEM I) Portland cement                 | 55%   | ↓ Declining   | HIGH       | EN 197-1                |
| CEM II/III (blended slag/fly-ash)           | 40%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | EU CO₂ taxonomy         |
| Calcined-clay (LC3) low-carbon cement       |  5%   | ↑ Emerging    | MEDIUM     | pilot projects          |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+

MV cable:
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
| Copper-XLPE (NA2XS2Y/NAYCWY DE std)         | 65%   | → Stable      | HIGH       | DIN VDE 0276-620        |
| Aluminum-XLPE (cost-driven long-run)        | 35%   | ↑ Growing     | HIGH       | DE DSO pref in rural    |
+---------------------------------------------+-------+---------------+------------+-------------------------+
```

---

sections.title: B.4 — Component Category Map
sections.content:
**Focal categories** that a cold-storage site engineer encounters across the BOM:

| Component category | L4 subsystem positions | Safety-critical? | Alternatives diverge? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Power semiconductor (IGBT/SiC) | S2 power stage · S3 protection relay firmware | YES | YES — SiC vs IGBT |
| Copper conductor | S2 filter · S3 transformer winding · S8 MV cable | NO | Cu vs Al (varies by position) |
| Lithium cell | S1 battery pack | YES | LFP vs NMC (converging to LFP) |
| Cooling compressor | S6 chiller · S5 HVAC (overlaps cold-storage main refrigeration!) | NO | Shared supplier base — refrigeration OEM overlap |
| Aerosol canister | S7 fire safety | YES | Single-source K-salt chemistry |
| PLC / edge controller | S4 EMS | NO | ARM SoC vs x86 vs DSP |
| Steel panel | S5 shell · S8 firewall | NO | Weathering vs galvanized vs stainless |

**Insight for cold-storage market:** The **refrigeration compressor category (L2)** appears both in the BESS thermal subsystem (S6) and in the warehouse's primary refrigeration plant. A cold-storage operator buying BESS from Mein Solar is also a buyer of large scroll/screw compressors — same supplier ecosystem (Copeland, BITZER, Bock), same refrigerant regulatory exposure (EU F-gas, R-454B/R-32 transition). This opens a conversation-adjacency Mein Solar can exploit in sales.

---

sections.title: B.5 — Company Position (already marked above)
sections.content:
Mein Solar sits at **L5** — the system integrator that delivers the commissioned asset. It does NOT sit inside a larger system's BOM (contrast with GfS Türwächter at L4). Therefore:

| BOM type | Produced? | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Upward context BOM | N/A | Mein Solar is not a component supplier; no parent system exists above the L5 BESS plant at a cold-storage site |
| Downward product BOM | **YES (this document)** | L5 → L0 decomposition of the BESS asset Mein Solar delivers |

---

sections.title: B.6 — Strategic BOM Adjacency (cold-storage context)
sections.content:
### Horizontal adjacency — other L4 subsystems Mein Solar could own

Mein Solar already owns all 8 L4 subsystems as an EPC integrator. Horizontal adjacency inside the BESS plant is saturated. The relevant horizontal move is **adjacent L5 systems at the same cold-storage site:**

```
+---------------------------------------------+------------------+---------------------+-------------------+
| Adjacent L5 system at cold-storage site     | Capability Gap   | Market Size (est.)  | Strategic Fit     |
+---------------------------------------------+------------------+---------------------+-------------------+
| Rooftop PV plant (>500 kW)                  | LOW              | €300k–1.5M/site     | STRONG            |
| HPC truck charging hub (BEV drayage)        | LOW-MED          | €500k–2M/site       | STRONG            |
| Refrigeration-plant energy retrofit         | HIGH             | €1–4M/site          | WEAK (not core)   |
| LED / controls retrofit                     | MEDIUM           | €100–400k/site      | MEDIUM            |
| CHP / biogas firming plant                  | HIGH             | €2–6M/site          | WEAK              |
+---------------------------------------------+------------------+---------------------+-------------------+
```

### Vertical move (up) — from L5 EPC to asset-owner / IPP

```
+---------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| Factor                                      | Evaluation                                |
+---------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
| Capabilities required                       | Asset-ownership finance, revenue trading, |
|                                             | long-term O&M, insurance wrap             |
| Capital investment                          | €250–400/kWh installed × MWh portfolio —  |
|                                             | €3–50M equity commitment                  |
| Customer-relationship leverage              | HIGH — already trusted single-source EPC  |
| Competitive response risk                   | MEDIUM — Voltfang / Kyon / Entrix already |
|                                             | offer "storage-as-a-service" in DE        |
| Time to market                              | 12–18 months to structure SPV + first fund|
+---------------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------+
```

**Verdict:** Plausible but capital-intensive. Deferred unless Mein Solar finds an IPP / fund partner. Revenue-stacking competence is the real gap.

### Vertical move (down) — supplying BESS *components* into other end-products

```
+-------------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-------------------+
| Target System                 | BOM Position        | Shared Tech          | Adaptation Needed |
+-------------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-------------------+
| Cold-storage refrigeration    | L4 compressor-pump  | thermal management   | refrigeration     |
| plant (on same site)          | controls            | know-how from S6     | domain expertise  |
| Industrial microgrid          | L4 full BESS sub   | entire offering      | islanding control |
| HPC charging hub              | L4 BESS buffer     | entire offering      | EV-charging SOP   |
| PV+wind hybrid park           | L4 BESS subsystem  | entire offering      | wind-site civil   |
| TSO Netzbooster               | L4 sub-100-MW BESS | ~50% of offering     | HV grid code,     |
|                               | block              |                      | consortium role   |
+-------------------------------+---------------------+----------------------+-------------------+
```

**Strongest cross-system leverage:** offering the *same BESS L5 asset* under different VN L6 contexts (microgrid, HPC hub). This is the FP-extension thesis from §02e, confirmed by the BOM.

### Adjacency grounded in the BOM (check)

All adjacencies above reference BOM positions present in sections S1–S8 (no speculative components). ✓

---

sections.title: Quality Checks
sections.content:
| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| NAICS routing applied (service sector → VN System BOM only, no end-product BOM) | ✅ |
| Every L4 traces to L0 through L3, L2, L1 | ✅ (S1–S8 all reach L0) |
| Every level transition has an alternatives table | ✅ |
| Single-source branches marked with `single-source: true` | ✅ (Cu-ETP, Al can, K-salt, Novec) |
| Shares sum to 100% (±1%) at every transition | ✅ |
| Alternatives = technology/material choices, not brands | ✅ |
| Legacy declining alternatives ≥2% included | ✅ (R-410A, Si-IGBT, SF6, mineral oil, etc.) |
| Company position marked (L5 integrator) | ✅ |
| Horizontal + vertical adjacency analysis | ✅ |
| BOM vs VN confusion test (does it end IN the asset?) | ✅ (all components end in the BESS plant, not machines that make it) |

---
